---
title: "Figure 3"
author: "Phil Zhou"
date: "2025-11-16"
output: html_document
---

```{r load_packages}
library(svglite)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
library(readxl)
library(tidyverse)
library(pheatmap)
library(viridis)
library(gplots)
library(zoo)
library(patchwork)
library(corrplot)
library(ggpubr)
library(tidyr)
library(here)
```

```{r load_dfs, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
#Load in the data
BEAN_FUSE_FL <- read_csv(
  here("data", "0326_LDLR_mastersheet.csv")
)

#Figure 3A
fig_3A_df <- read.csv(
  here("data", "LDLR_FL", "Fig3A_editfrac.csv")
)

#Figure 3B
fig_3B_df <- read_xlsx(
  here("data", "LDLR_FL", "0125_HCT116_LDLRPE_datasum.xlsx")
)

#Figure 3C
fig_3C_df <- read_xlsx(
  here("data", "LDLR_FL", "0125_HCT116_LDLRPE_datasum.xlsx")
)

#Figure 3D
fig_3D_df <- read_csv(
  here("data", "LDLR_peDMS_full_data_040126.csv")
)

```

```{r shared_theme}
#Set up shared theme for generating scatterplots 
panel_theme <- theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.line = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.8),
    plot.margin = margin(6, 6, 6, 6),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 18, color = "black"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 18, color = "black"),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 16, color = "black"),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 16, color = "black")
  )
```

```{r fig_3A}
# ---- Plotting Figure 3A ----
fig_3A <- fig_3A_df %>%
  mutate(BEAN_edit_percentage = BEAN.edit.fraction * 100) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = BEAN_edit_percentage)) +
  geom_histogram(
    binwidth = 2, boundary = 0,
    color = "grey", fill = "black", linewidth = 0.3
  ) +
  labs(y = "Number of pegRNAs", x = "LDLR-FL reporter edit percentage") +
  panel_theme +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05)))

#Printing out figure panel 
fig_3A

#save figure panel
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(here("output", "figure_3"), "Fig3A.pdf"),
  plot     = fig_3A,
  width    = 6,
  height   = 4,
  units    = "in",
  dpi = 600
)
```

```{r fig_3B}
fig_3B_df <- fig_3B_df %>%
  mutate(BEAN_edit_percentage = `BEAN edit fraction` * 100)

# ---- Plotting Figure 3B ----
fig_3B <- ggplot(fig_3B_df,
    aes(
      x = BEAN_edit_percentage,
      y = `PRIDICT2_0_editing_Score_deep_HEK`,
      color = RTlength
    )
  ) +
  geom_point(shape = 16, alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +
  scale_color_viridis_c() +
  labs(
    y = "PRIDICT2.0 score (HEK293T)",
    x = "LDLR-FL reporter edit percentage",
    color = "RT template length (nt)"
  ) +
  panel_theme +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.margin = margin(r = 8)
  )

#Printing out figure panel 
fig_3B

# ---- Calculating correlations for Figure 3B ----
# Pearson correlation
cor(fig_3B_df$`BEAN_edit_percentage`, fig_3B_df$`PRIDICT2_0_editing_Score_deep_HEK`, method = "pearson", "complete.obs")
# Spearman correlation
cor(fig_3B_df$`BEAN_edit_percentage`, fig_3B_df$`PRIDICT2_0_editing_Score_deep_HEK`, method = "spearman", "complete.obs")

#6004-309 = 5695

#save figure panel
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(here("output", "figure_3"), "Fig3B.pdf"),
  plot     = fig_3B,
  width    = 6,
  height   = 4,
  units    = "in",
  dpi = 600
)
```

```{r fig_3C}
HCT116_LDLRPE_datasum <- fig_3B_df %>%
  mutate(BEAN_edit_percentage = `BEAN edit fraction` * 100)

sum_rt <- HCT116_LDLRPE_datasum %>%
  group_by(RTlength) %>%
  summarise(
    n    = n(),
    mean = mean(BEAN_edit_percentage, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd   = sd(BEAN_edit_percentage,  na.rm = TRUE),
    se   = sd / sqrt(n),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ---- Plotting Figure 3C ----
fig_3C <- ggplot(sum_rt, aes(x = RTlength, y = mean)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean - se, ymax = mean + se),
                width = 0, linewidth = 0.6, color = "black") +
  geom_point(size = 2.2, shape = 16, color = "black") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = sort(unique(sum_rt$RTlength))) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.03))) +
  labs(x = "pegRNA RT template length", y = "LDLR-FL reporter edit percentage") +
  panel_theme +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9))  # tighter ticks

#Printing out figure panel 
fig_3C

# ---- Calculating correlations for Figure 3C ----
# Pearson correlation
cor(HCT116_LDLRPE_datasum$`BEAN_edit_percentage`, HCT116_LDLRPE_datasum$`RTlength`, method = "pearson", "complete.obs")
# Spearman correlation
cor(HCT116_LDLRPE_datasum$`BEAN_edit_percentage`, HCT116_LDLRPE_datasum$`RTlength`, method = "spearman", "complete.obs")

#5696 entries 

#save figure panel
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(here("output", "figure_3"), "Fig3C.pdf"),
  plot     = fig_3C,
  width    = 6,
  height   = 6,
  units    = "in",
  dpi = 600
)
```

```{r fig_3D}
# ---- statistical testing for Figure 3D ----
comparisons_3d <- list(
  c("Control",    "Synonymous"),
  c("Synonymous", "Missense"),
  c("Missense",   "Stop"),
  c("Control",    "Missense"),
  c("Synonymous", "Stop"),
  c("Control",    "Stop")
)

fig_3D_long <- fig_3D_df %>%
  select(Variant.type, raw_score_scaled_by_negCtrl) %>%
  rename(
    Variant = Variant.type,
    Score   = raw_score_scaled_by_negCtrl
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Variant = factor(
      Variant,
      levels = c("negcontrol", "synonymous", "missense", "stop"),
      labels = c("Control", "Synonymous", "Missense", "Stop")
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Score), is.finite(Score))

# Wilcoxon tests with Holm correction
wilcox_test <- compare_means(
  Score ~ Variant,
  data = fig_3D_long,
  method = "wilcox.test",
  method.args = list(var.equal = FALSE),
  comparisons = comparisons_3d,
  p.adjust.method = "holm"
)

# Pairwise Welch t-tests with Holm correction
t_test <- compare_means(
  Score ~ Variant,
  data = fig_3D_long,
  method = "t.test",
  method.args = list(var.equal = FALSE),
  comparisons = comparisons_3d,
  p.adjust.method = "holm"
)

# Dynamic y positions
ymax   <- max(fig_3D_long$Score, na.rm = TRUE)
ymin   <- min(fig_3D_long$Score, na.rm = TRUE)
yrange <- ymax - ymin

# If yrange is zero for some reason, avoid zero spacing
if (yrange == 0) yrange <- 1

t_test <- t_test %>%
  mutate(
    p.signif = case_when(
      p.adj <= 0.0001 ~ "****",
      p.adj <= 0.001  ~ "***",
      p.adj <= 0.01   ~ "**",
      p.adj <= 0.05   ~ "*",
      TRUE            ~ "n.s."
    ),
    y.position = ymax + c(0.06, 0.14, 0.22, 0.30, 0.38, 0.46) * yrange
  )

# ---- Plotting Figure 3D ----
fig_3D <- ggplot(fig_3D_long, aes(x = Variant, y = Score, fill = Variant)) +
  geom_point( #Scatterplot
    aes(color = Variant),
    position = position_jitter(width = 0.05, height = 0),
    size = 1.2, alpha = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = 0,
    linetype = "dotted",
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_violin(alpha = 0.5, trim = FALSE) + #Violin plot
  geom_boxplot( #Boxplot
    width = 0.15,
    outlier.shape = NA,
    alpha = 0.7,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "LDLR-FL BEAN z score") +
  coord_cartesian(
    ylim = c(ymin - 0.05 * yrange, ymax + 0.55 * yrange)
  ) +
  panel_theme +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  scale_fill_discrete(guide = "none") +
  scale_color_discrete(guide = "none") +
  stat_pvalue_manual(
    t_test,
    label = "p.signif",
    xmin = "group1",
    xmax = "group2",
    y.position = "y.position",
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    hide.ns = FALSE,
    tip.length = 0.01,
    size = 8,
    bracket.size = 0.6,
    color = "black"
  )

#Printing out figure panel 
fig_3D

#print out results of statistical test
t_test
wilcox_test

#save figure panel
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(here("output", "figure_3"), "Fig3D.pdf"),
  plot     = fig_3D,
  width    = 6,
  height   = 6,
  units    = "in",
  dpi = 600
)
```
```{r fig_3E}
#Add label for whether the starting amino acid is a cysteine or not
#Variants corresponding to cysteine mutants are colored red

  BEAN_FUSE_FL_clean <- BEAN_FUSE_FL %>%
    filter(!is.na(`Differential PFAM`), `Differential PFAM` != 0) %>%
    mutate(
      is_cysteine = `Starting AA` == "C",
      Variant_name = paste0(
        `Starting AA`,
        `Position`,
        `Final AA`
      )
    )

# ---- Plotting Figure 3E ----
fig_3E <- ggplot(
  BEAN_FUSE_FL_clean,
  aes(x = `LDLR-FL FUSE_score normalized`, y = `Differential PFAM`, color = is_cysteine)
) +
  geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.7, shape = 16) +
  scale_color_manual(
  values = c(`TRUE` = "red", `FALSE` = "black"),
  guide = "none"
) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(-0.5, 1.5, by = 0.5)  # force 0, 0.5, 1.0, 1.5
  ) +
theme(legend.position = "none") + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", color = "black", linewidth = 0.6) +
  labs(y = "Differential PFAM", x = "LDLR-FL FUSE score", color = NULL) +
  panel_theme +                    # already has legend.position = "none"
  theme(legend.position = "none")

#4921-3629 = 1292
#Recalculate correlations 

# ---- Calculating correlations for Figure 3E ----
cor(BEAN_FUSE_FL$`LDLR-FL FUSE_score normalized`, BEAN_FUSE_FL$`Differential PFAM`, method = "pearson", "complete.obs")
cor(BEAN_FUSE_FL$`LDLR-FL FUSE_score normalized`, BEAN_FUSE_FL$`Differential PFAM`, method = "spearman", "complete.obs")

#Printing out figure panel 
fig_3E

#save figure panel
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(here("output", "figure_3"), "Fig3E.pdf"),
  plot     = fig_3E,
  width    = 6,
  height   = 4,
  units    = "in",
  dpi = 600
)
```

```{r fig_3F}
#Add label for whether the starting amino acid is a cysteine or not
#Variants corresponding to cysteine mutants are colored red

fig_3F_clean <- BEAN_FUSE_FL %>%
  filter(!is.na(AlphaMissense), !is.na(`LDLR-FL FUSE`)) %>%
  mutate(
    is_cysteine = `Starting AA` == "C",
    Variant_name = paste0(
      `Starting AA`,
      `Position`,
      `Final AA`
    )
  )

# ---- Plotting Figure 3F ----
fig_3F <- ggplot(
  fig_3F_clean,
  aes(x = `LDLR-FL FUSE_score normalized`, y = AlphaMissense, color = is_cysteine)
) +
  geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.7, shape = 16) +
  scale_color_manual(
  values = c(`TRUE` = "red", `FALSE` = "black"),
  guide = "none"
) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(-0.5, 1.5, by = 0.5)  # force 0, 0.5, 1.0, 1.5
  ) +
  
  labs(
    y = "AlphaMissense score",
    x = "LDLR-FL FUSE score",
    color = NULL
  ) +
  panel_theme +               # use the same boxed theme
  theme(legend.position = "none")   

# ---- Calculating correlations for Figure 3F ----
cor(BEAN_FUSE_FL$`LDLR-FL FUSE_score normalized`, BEAN_FUSE_FL$`AlphaMissense`, method = "pearson", "complete.obs")
cor(BEAN_FUSE_FL$`LDLR-FL FUSE_score normalized`, BEAN_FUSE_FL$`AlphaMissense`, method = "spearman", "complete.obs")

#Printing out figure panel 
fig_3F

#save figure panel
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(here("output", "figure_3"), "Fig3F.pdf"),
  plot     = fig_3F,
  width    = 6,
  height   = 4,
  units    = "in",
  dpi = 600
)
```

```{r printing_fig, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# Consistent small per-panel margin to avoid clipping 
panel_theme_boxed <- panel_theme + 
  theme(
    plot.margin = margin(4, 4, 4, 4),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 1),
    axis.line = element_blank()
  )

# Apply to all panels (keeps borders aligned)
fig_3A <- fig_3A + panel_theme_boxed
fig_3B <- fig_3B + panel_theme_boxed + theme(legend.position = "right")  # legend shown here
fig_3C <- fig_3C + panel_theme_boxed
fig_3D <- fig_3D + panel_theme_boxed
fig_3E <- fig_3E + panel_theme_boxed
fig_3F <- fig_3F + panel_theme_boxed

# Arrange 3×2 with gutters (A,B,C / D,E,F)
hgap <- 0.08  # horizontal gutter (relative width of spacers)
vgap <- 0.10  # vertical gutter (relative height of spacer row)

row_top <- (fig_3A | plot_spacer() | fig_3B | plot_spacer() | fig_3C) +
  plot_layout(widths = c(1, hgap, 1, hgap, 1))

row_bot <- (fig_3D | plot_spacer() | fig_3E | plot_spacer() | fig_3F) +
  plot_layout(widths = c(1, hgap, 1, hgap, 1))

fig_3 <- row_top / plot_spacer() / row_bot +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1, vgap, 1), guides = "collect") &   # collect legend globally
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",               # single legend on the figure right
    legend.margin   = margin(6, 6, 6, 6),
    plot.margin     = margin(8, 8, 8, 8),     # outer breathing room
  )

#Printing out figure panel 
fig_3

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(here("output", "figure_3"), "Fig3.pdf"),
  plot     = fig_3,
  width    = 36,
  height   = 12,
  units    = "in",
  create.dir = TRUE,
  dpi = 600
)
```
